Who We Are

Built for busy people.
With real lives.

ECPLABS started with one question: Why is supplementing so complicated? CRE18 is the answer — a precision-crafted formula featuring 4 forms of creatine, a full vitamin complex, electrolytes, collagen peptides, and green tea extract caffeine, all in one tear-and-go stick pack.

No tubs. No scoops. No compromise. Just a complete daily formula in flavors you'll actually look forward to.

How It Works

1
Creatine Benefits
＋
When you work out, your muscles burn through energy fast. Creatine helps your body recharge that energy so you can train harder and recover faster. Think of it like a backup battery for your muscles.

CRE18 uses 4 different forms of creatine:

Creatine Monohydrate — the most studied form. Builds up in your muscles over time and could help support strength, power, and endurance.

Creatine Pyruvate — gets into your bloodstream faster, so your muscles could get more creatine available during your workout.

Creatine Citrate — the easy-on-the-stomach version. Same benefits as monohydrate, but gentler on digestion and mixes more easily.

Magnesium Creatine Chelate — creatine bonded to magnesium, the mineral your body needs to actually use the energy creatine provides. Could help support better absorption, less cramping, and faster recovery.

Bonus: Emerging research suggests creatine may also help support women during menopause — contributing to muscle maintenance, bone strength, and mental sharpness during hormonal changes.
2
Focus & Mental Clarity
＋
Cognitive performance during exercise and daily activity is governed largely by neurotransmitter signaling and cerebral blood flow. CRE18 includes 50mg of caffeine sourced from green tea extract, which works by binding to adenosine receptors in the brain — blocking the chemical responsible for inducing fatigue and drowsiness — resulting in increased alertness and reaction time. Unlike synthetic caffeine, green tea extract also contains catechins (EGCG) which act as neuroprotective antioxidants. Creatine itself has also been shown in emerging research to support phosphocreatine availability in the brain, which could help support cognitive resilience under mental fatigue. Vitamin B6 and B12 contribute to neurotransmitter synthesis — including dopamine and serotonin — supporting mood, focus, and mental processing throughout the day.
3
Energy & Recovery
＋
Energy production at a cellular level is a multi-step biochemical process. B vitamins act as essential coenzymes in the Krebs cycle — the cellular pathway that converts carbohydrates, fats, and proteins into usable ATP. Without sufficient B vitamins (B1, B2, B3, B5), this conversion process becomes inefficient, resulting in fatigue. Magnesium plays a parallel role as a cofactor in over 300 enzymatic reactions, including those governing muscle relaxation and nervous system regulation after exertion. Electrolytes — sodium, potassium, and chloride — maintain the electrochemical gradients across cell membranes that allow muscles to contract and nerves to fire. When electrolytes are depleted through sweat, muscle cramping and impaired performance follow. CRE18 replenishes all of these simultaneously, supporting sustained energy and faster physiological recovery.
4
Vitamins & Immunity
＋
The immune system depends on a network of micronutrients to function properly. Vitamin C is a potent water-soluble antioxidant that neutralizes free radicals generated during exercise-induced oxidative stress — free radicals that can damage cells if left unchecked. Vitamin D binds to receptors on immune cells and regulates the expression of antimicrobial proteins that are part of the body's first line of defense. Zinc is required for the development and activation of T-lymphocytes — the white blood cells responsible for identifying and eliminating pathogens. Vitamin E provides fat-soluble antioxidant protection, working alongside Vitamin C to form a layered cellular defense system. B9 (Folate) and B12 support red blood cell formation and DNA replication — critical for the rapid cell division required when mounting an immune response. Together these nutrients could help support a well-regulated, responsive immune system.
5
Skin, Hair & Nails
＋
Skin, hair, and nail health are directly tied to the structural proteins and nutrients that maintain their integrity at a cellular level. Collagen is the most abundant protein in the body, forming the scaffolding of skin dermis and the matrix of nails and hair follicle support tissue. As collagen production naturally declines with age, supplementing with hydrolyzed Collagen Peptides provides the specific amino acids — glycine, proline, and hydroxyproline — that could help support the body's ability to synthesize new collagen. Biotin (B7) serves as a coenzyme in the metabolism of amino acids and fatty acids that are building blocks for keratin — the structural protein of hair and nails. Vitamin C is essential to collagen synthesis as it acts as a cofactor for the enzymes that stabilize the collagen triple helix structure. Zinc contributes to keratinocyte function — the cells responsible for forming the outer layer of skin and the hair shaft.

What's Inside

Every ingredient. Every reason.

Creatine Complex
The gold standard. Could help support muscle fuel through Adenosine Triphosphate (ATP) — your body's energy molecule — so you can push harder, recover faster, and build strength over time.
Could help support faster creatine absorption. May help sustain energy, helps reduce fatigue during longer workouts, and pairs perfectly with monohydrate.
The easy-on-the-stomach form. Could help support strength and power — mixes instantly and absorbs quickly — without the bloating some people experience with standard creatine.
Creatine bonded to magnesium — the mineral your body needs to activate Adenosine Triphosphate (ATP) for energy. May support better absorption, less cramping, and faster recovery after training.
Energy & Hydration
Could help support fluid balance, proper muscle contractions, and sustain nerve signaling — helping you stay hydrated and perform at your best.
Could help support smooth, focused energy without the hard crash — sourced from natural green tea extract of synthetic caffeine. Also delivers catechins (EGCG) — plant antioxidants that help protect cells from oxidative stress. Available in 50mg, 100mg, and 150mg — or 0mg caffeine free.
Vitamin Complex
Could help support normal immune function, healthy vision, and cell growth. Also plays a role in maintaining the integrity of skin and mucous membranes as part of the body's natural barrier.
B1 — energy metabolism & nerve function.
B2 — releases energy from food at a cellular level.
B3 — energy production & normal metabolism.
B5 — energy-yielding metabolism & hormone synthesis.
B6 — protein metabolism & neurotransmitter production.
B7 (Biotin) — supports maintenance of hair & nails.
B9 (Folate) — cell production & DNA synthesis.
B12 — red blood cell formation, nerve function & reduces fatigue.
Could help support immune function as a potent antioxidant and helps protect cells from oxidative stress. Also essential for collagen synthesis — making it a natural complement to the collagen peptides in this formula.
Could help support healthy bones and teeth by aiding calcium absorption. Also contributes to normal immune function, muscle function, and plays a role in mood regulation — particularly important for those with limited sun exposure.
Could help support protection of cells from oxidative damage as a fat-soluble antioxidant caused by exercise and daily stress. Works alongside Vitamin C as part of the body's natural antioxidant defense system.
Minerals & Support
Involved in over 300 enzymatic reactions. Could help support Adenosine Triphosphate (ATP) production, muscle relaxation and recovery, normal nerve function, and may support sleep quality — a foundational ingredient for active individuals.
Could help support normal immune function, protein and DNA synthesis, and contributes to the maintenance of normal testosterone levels. Also plays a role in wound healing and helps protect cells from oxidative stress.
Hydrolyzed collagen that is easily absorbed. Could help support skin hydration and elasticity, and contribute to nail strength, and provide amino acids that are building blocks for hair structure — with benefits appearing after consistent daily use over several weeks.

Who It's For

CRE18 works at every age.

🔥 18 – 25 The Grind Years Tap to learn more ›
  • Could help support muscle growth and strength gains during peak athletic development
  • Could help replenish Adenosine Triphosphate (ATP) for high-intensity training and competition
  • Could help support sprint speed and power output, and performance in team sports
  • Could help support faster recovery between sessions so you can train harder, more often
  • B vitamins and green tea extract support sustained energy and mental focus for long study or training days
    • ⚡ 26 – 35 Peak Performance Tap to learn more ›
  • Could help support lean muscle mass as natural creatine production begins to gradually decline
  • Could help support strength, power, and endurance during your busiest and most demanding years
  • Collagen peptides could help support joint comfort and connective tissue maintenance for active lifestyles
  • Electrolytes and magnesium could help support hydration and recovery for those balancing career, family, and fitness
  • Green tea extract provides clean, focused energy without over-stimulation
    • 💪 36 – 50 Stay Strong Tap to learn more ›
  • Could help support the maintenance of muscle mass as the body naturally begins to lose muscle with age (sarcopenia)
  • Creatine combined with resistance training may help slow age-related strength decline
  • Vitamin D and magnesium could help support bone density and joint function as the body ages
  • Emerging research suggests creatine may support cognitive function and mental sharpness during this decade
  • For women: could help support muscle and energy during perimenopause as hormone levels begin to shift
  • Collagen peptides support skin elasticity and nail strength that naturally decrease with age
    • 🌟 51 – 65 Vitality & Longevity Tap to learn more ›
  • Creatine combined with resistance training could help support strength and muscle maintenance in older adults beyond what training alone achieves
  • For women in menopause: could help support muscle mass, bone health, and cognitive function during significant hormonal changes
  • Zinc and vitamin D could help support immune function which becomes increasingly important with age
  • B12 could help support nerve function and energy — absorption naturally decreases after 50
  • Collagen peptides could help support joint comfort and skin hydration
  • Low-dose caffeine from green tea extract could help support mental alertness without overstimulating a more sensitive system
    • 🏆 65+ Active Aging Tap to learn more ›
  • Could help support the fight against sarcopenia — the age-related loss of muscle mass and strength that can significantly impact quality of life
  • Creatine with resistance exercise could help support improved strength in older adults more than exercise alone
  • Magnesium and electrolytes could help support muscle function, hydration, and may help reduce the risk of cramping
  • Vitamin D could help support calcium absorption and bone health — critical for reducing fracture risk in later years
  • B vitamins could help support energy metabolism and nerve function, helping maintain daily vitality
  • Collagen peptides could help support skin, nail, and joint health as collagen production continues to decline
  • Always consult a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement routine

      Tap to see benefits ›
    • Muscle Strength & Power Creatine could help support increases in strength, explosive power, and high-intensity output — helping you lift more, run faster, and perform better.
    • Testosterone Support Zinc could help support the maintenance of normal testosterone levels — an important factor in muscle growth, energy, and overall male health.
    • Faster Recovery Magnesium could help support muscle relaxation and recovery after training. Electrolytes replace what's lost through sweat so you bounce back faster between sessions.
    • Lean Muscle Maintenance The 4-form creatine blend could help support muscle mass over time — especially important as natural creatine production decreases with age.
    • Cognitive Performance 50mg of natural green tea extract caffeine could help support sharp focus and mental clarity during training and throughout your day without overstimulation.
    • Immune & Cellular Health A full vitamin complex including Vitamins C, D, E, and Zinc could help support immune function and antioxidant protection, and overall daily wellness.
    • Skin, Hair & Nail Support Collagen Peptides could help support skin hydration, elasticity, and nail strength. Biotin (B7) could help support the maintenance of healthy hair and nails with consistent daily use.
    • Menopause Support Emerging research suggests creatine may help support muscle mass, bone density, and cognitive function during the hormonal changes that come with menopause — offering benefits beyond the gym.
    • Lean Muscle & Tone Creatine could help support muscle strength and lean mass without causing unwanted bulk — helping women build and maintain a strong, toned physique alongside regular exercise.
    • Energy & Focus B vitamins could help support energy metabolism and reduce tiredness and fatigue. Green tea extract caffeine provides a smooth, sustained energy boost without the jitters.
    • Bone & Immune Health Vitamin D could help support calcium absorption for healthy bones — especially important for women. Vitamin C and Zinc support immune function and antioxidant protection.
    • Hydration & Recovery Electrolytes and magnesium could help support fluid balance, muscle function, and recovery — helping you feel your best inside and outside the gym.
